title: BEAD-05 [review] needs-ordering, skipped-required, verdict discriminator, no BDL-049 regression

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
REVIEW (BEAD-05 / BDL-050 CI consolidation) — read-only quality/architecture/honesty review of the W1-W3 diff.

VERDICT: PASS-WITH-FIXES (0 critical, 0 major, 2 minor, 1 nitpick). Suite green (3653 passed), mypy/ruff/lint --strict all clean, all 6 workflow/template YAMLs yaml.safe_load cleanly.

=== DIMENSION 1 — skipped-required (the crux): SOUND. ===
Traced ci.yml: gate / tests / site-build have NO job-level `if:` — they run UNCONDITIONALLY on every pull_request AND workflow_dispatch. ai-techwriter is the ONLY conditionally-skipped job (needs:[gate,tests,site-build]). So:
- red gate/test/site ⇒ ai-techwriter SKIPPED ⇒ PR blocked BY THE RED UPSTREAM (GitHub treats a skipped required check as neutral/passing; the red required upstream is what blocks). Correct.
- There is NO path where all three verify jobs are skipped-together ⇒ ai-techwriter skipped ⇒ everything neutral ⇒ accidentally mergeable. The three have no `if`, no `needs`, no paths filter — they cannot be collectively skipped. The mis-gate failure mode the RFC worried about is structurally absent. The dogfood (.6) should still confirm GitHub's skipped-required semantics live, but the design is correct by construction.

=== DIMENSION 2 — verdict discriminator honesty: SOUND + actually conservative. ===
classify_verdict (runner.py:48-72): tokens>0 ⇒ flagged(block); ==0 ⇒ infra(pass). Checked the "real doc failure silently misclassified as infra" attack:
- Tokens are accumulated in _repair_one_doc (runner.py:249) BEFORE publish. So any agent that produced output ⇒ flagged stands even if a later step fails.
- "agent edits a doc, produces tokens, but publish/commit fails with 0 tokens": cannot happen — the publish failure path (_push_current_branch, seams.py:469) RAISES RuntimeError, which propagates out of run_harness → uncaught in cli.main → hard non-zero CI exit (RED), not a silent infra-pass. Conservative.
- "gate-red but tokens happened to be 0": only reachable if the agent never produced a single token across every attempt (true infra) — in which case classifying infra is correct.
- Loudness: infra emits `::warning title=AI tech-writer::` (cli.py:265) ALWAYS + best-effort PR comment; a wrongly-passed infra is visible. The verdict truth table is unit-tested incl. the exact boundaries (flagged-1in→1, flagged-1out→1, infra-0tokens→0+warning; test_bdl050_hardening.py:343-389). Genuinely conservative.

=== DIMENSION 3 — AI-TW required-but-infra-passes: CORRECT. ===
cli._report: ok/infra→exit0, flagged→Exit(1) (cli.py:243-251). A dead VPS / exhausted $30 quota ⇒ tokens==0 ⇒ infra ⇒ exit0 ⇒ does NOT freeze merges; genuine unresolved drift ⇒ flagged ⇒ red. Exit-code↔conclusion mapping verified.

=== DIMENSION 4 — no BDL-049 regression: PRESERVED 1:1. ===
Diffed the old ai-techwriter.yml job body vs the ci.yml ai-techwriter job: the ONLY invariant-line delta is the intended setup-uv@v5→@v6 Node24 bump. loop-guard, AI_TW_PAT checkout (token: AI_TW_PAT||github.token), ref: PR head, fetch-depth:0, merge-base --since w/ BASE_SHA fallback, --target pr-branch, GH_TOKEN, PR_URL, branch-pr workflow_dispatch fallback — all byte-identical. concurrency group correctly renamed ai-techwriter-* → ci-* (single workflow now), cancel-in-progress:true kept. GitLab mirror (.gitlab-ci.yml) mirrors structure (stages verify→docs, needs:[gate,tests,site-build], merge_request_event rule, CI_JOB_TOKEN/AI_TW_PAT push, merge-base --since w/ DIFF_BASE_SHA fallback).

=== DIMENSION 5 — deletions safe: YES. ===
beadloom-gate.yml/tests.yml/ai-techwriter.yml deleted; folded into ci.yml. pypi-publish.yml correctly INLINES the 3.10-3.13 matrix (was workflow_call into tests.yml) — release pipeline self-contained. deploy-site.yml still on push:branches:[main] + workflow_dispatch (the only push:main job). test_no_workflow_references_deleted_tests_yml guards stale references.

=== DIMENSION 6 — branch-protection check names: MATCH (but see MINOR-1). ===
DEFAULT_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS = (gate, tests (3.10..3.13), site-build, ai-techwriter) — manually verified these EXACTLY match ci.yml's job names + the rendered matrix-leg check-run names (tests strategy.matrix.python-version → "tests (3.x)"). enforce_admins:true + 0 reviews + un-filtered always-running checks ⇒ owner self-merges, not locked out. No typo found.

=== DIMENSION 7 — Node24: REASONABLE. ===
checkout@v5, setup-uv@v6, setup-node@v5 run natively on Node24. deploy-site node-version 18→22. Pages actions (configure-pages@v5/upload-pages-artifact@v3/deploy-pages@v4 — current majors, still Node20 entrypoints) opted into Node24 via env:FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24:true — a documented, scoped stopgap. test_actions_are_node24_majors guards the tags.

=== DIMENSION 8 — architecture/security: CLEAN. ===
DDD intact (lint --strict exit 0; only domain-size-limit warnings on graph/application, pre-existing, severity warn). No Any/type:ignore w/o reason. No bare except (best-effort paths use (RuntimeError, OSError)). subprocess argv-built, never shell=True; branch_protection _subprocess_runner has a justified noqa:S603. No print/breakpoint in touched python.

----- FINDINGS -----
MINOR-1 (test-gap, dimension 6 lockout): There is NO automated cross-check tying branch_protection.DEFAULT_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS to the ACTUAL rendered ci.yml job/matrix check-run names. test_branch_protection.py asserts a hardcoded literal list; test_ci_consolidated*.py asserts ci.yml has those jobs — but independently, against separate literals. A future rename of a ci.yml job (or matrix leg) would silently desync the required-check set ⇒ permanently-unmergeable main, with both test suites still green. RECOMMEND: add one test that derives the expected contexts FROM ci.yml (parse jobs + matrix legs) and asserts == DEFAULT_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS. (Not blocking now: names manually verified to match.)

MINOR-2 (doc freshness): docs/guides/agentic-flow.md:42 still says "the always-on `beadloom-gate` check required" — the OLD pre-BDL-050 required-check name. After this bead the required check is `gate` (+ tests/site-build/ai-techwriter). The authoritative onboarding README + cli.md are updated; this guide prose is stale. RECOMMEND: tech-writer wave (W6) updates beadloom-gate→gate here. (sync-check is [ok] — prose drift it doesn't track.)

NITPICK: ci.yml gate job uses the composite ./.github/actions/beadloom-gate Action while the VENDORED github-workflow.yml template runs `beadloom ci --format github` directly via uv. Intentional (the repo dogfoods its own composite Action; adopters have no such Action) and documented, but worth a one-line note in ci.yml that this divergence from the template is deliberate.

OVERALL: PASS-WITH-FIXES. No critical/major. The two crux dimensions (skipped-required ordering + verdict misclassification) are both SOUND and genuinely conservative. MINOR-1 (add a ci.yml↔contexts derive-and-assert test) is the most valuable follow-up (closes the only real lockout-regression hole); MINOR-2 is a one-word tech-writer fix. Neither blocks the dogfood.

title: BEAD-05 [review] loop-guard correctness, no-auto-merge, merge-base, branch-protection owner-safe

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
REVIEW (BEAD-05) — BDL-049 W1-W3 quality/architecture/honesty review. READ-ONLY.

VERDICT: PASS-WITH-FIXES. Suite green (3568 passed), mypy src/+tools/ clean, ruff clean, beadloom lint --strict exit 0 (only pre-existing domain-size cardinality WARNs). The loop-guard is correctly designed and closed (see D1). One MAJOR: the branch-protection DEFAULT status-check contexts do not match this repo's actual GitHub check-run names — applying with defaults makes main's required checks unsatisfiable.

=== DIMENSION 1 — LOOP-GUARD (the #1 risk): CLOSED. ===
Trace verified end-to-end. Agent commit is authored by beadloom-ai-techwriter AND its subject STARTS with [skip ai-techwriter] (seams.py:390 _commit_to_current_branch -> message=f"{_SKIP_TOKEN} {title}"). On the agent's push, synchronize fires; the workflow checks out ref: github.event.pull_request.head.ref with fetch-depth:0 (ai-techwriter.yml:50-51) — on synchronize GitHub advances head to the just-pushed commit, so the branch-name checkout resolves to the agent's HEAD, and git log -1 (loop-guard step, :56-64) reads THAT commit. Either condition (author==beadloom-ai-techwriter OR subject contains [skip ai-techwriter]) sets AI_TW_SKIP=1; every subsequent step is gated `if: env.AI_TW_SKIP != '1'` (verified by test_github_every_post_guard_step_is_gated_on_skip, which asserts EVERY post-guard step carries the gate). GitLab mirror uses `exit 0` after the same probe (.gitlab-ci.yml:35-41). Belt-and-suspenders (author + token) is real.
Robustness notes (all acceptable, no action needed):
 - Substring match grep -qF '[skip ai-techwriter]' is fixed-string (no regex foot-gun). A human commit would only match if they literally type the token — acceptable/intended escape hatch.
 - Squash/merge of the PR happens on MAIN (push:main trigger was REMOVED), so it cannot retrigger this workflow. Force-push by the agent is a plain push (git push origin HEAD), still authored by the bot -> still guarded.
 - The author check is the durable guard even if a future rebase/amend drops the token from the subject.

=== DIMENSION 2 — NO-AUTO-MERGE: PRESERVED. ===
The pr-branch publishers only commit-to-current-branch + push + post a PR/MR comment (seams.py GitHubPRBranchPublisher/GitLabPRBranchPublisher). No gh pr merge / glab mr merge anywhere. Branch protection requires a PR (0 reviews) but the human still clicks merge. Confirmed.

=== DIMENSION 3 — --since merge-base: ROBUST. ===
since="$(git merge-base "origin/$BASE_REF" HEAD 2>/dev/null || true)"; if empty -> $BASE_SHA fallback (ai-techwriter.yml:105-106; GitLab :59-60 -> $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DIFF_BASE_SHA). fetch-depth:0 ensures origin/$BASE is present on the self-hosted runner. cli.py _normalize_since drops empty/all-zero SHAs to None (stored-state path) as a third line of defense. Behind/ahead/force-push all handled. Good.

=== DIMENSION 4 — pr-branch publisher: CORRECT. ===
Commits to CURRENT branch, never checkout -b (test asserts no 'checkout' arg at all). Plain push git push origin HEAD (NOT force) — fast-forward of the runner's PR-head checkout (correct; force is only the bot-owned regenerated branch of the legacy branch-pr path). 0-doc no-op truly makes NO commit and NO push (_has_doc_changes probe via git diff --cached --quiet -- docs; test_github_pr_branch_zero_docs_skips_empty_commit). Comment is best-effort (logged + swallowed; test_*_comment_failure_does_not_fail_run). pr_url read from PR_URL env / composed from CI_MERGE_REQUEST_* (empty-safe via _gitlab_mr_url). A genuine push failure DOES fail the run (test_github_pr_branch_raises_on_push_failure). Note: the commit/push happen relative to the runner's checkout, which is the branch (not detached) because ref is head.ref (a branch name), not head.sha — correct for committing.

=== DIMENSION 6 — FLOW 1:1 + DRIFT-GUARD: OK. ===
agentic_flow vendored coordinator.md is BYTE-IDENTICAL to live .claude/commands/coordinator.md; CLAUDE.md.txt differs ONLY by the __BEADLOOM_PROJECT_NAME__ placeholder vs "Beadloom" (the expected templating normalization — drift-guard is real and green). CLAUDE.md §6 + coordinator edits are additive (trunk-based flow added; the wave/gate protocol untouched). The ai_techwriter/*.yml CI templates are intentionally NOT byte-match drift-guarded (separate vendored copies); they differ from live only in comment text and are validated SEMANTICALLY by test_ci_pr_trigger.py (pull_request present, push absent, --target pr-branch, merge_request_event). Acceptable by design.

=== DIMENSION 7 — ARCHITECTURE / SECURITY: CLEAN. ===
DDD: branch_protection.py lives in onboarding (annotated # beadloom:domain=onboarding), no infra import, lint --strict green. subprocess argv is fully list-form everywhere (no shell=True); _subprocess_runner has a justified noqa:S603 (argv built internally). gh pr comment / glab mr note take the PR title/body as separate argv elements — no shell-injection via branch names or PR titles. yaml.safe_load used in tests. _SKIP_TOKEN carries a justified noqa:S105. No bare except (only RuntimeError/OSError caught in _backfill_pr_url). No unjustified Any/type:ignore. mypy --strict clean on src/+tools/.

=== DIMENSION 8 — BOOTSTRAP: SOUND, with a noted gotcha for .6. ===
GitHub runs pull_request workflows from the BASE (main) version of the workflow file. So this NEW ai-techwriter.yml (pull_request trigger + pr-branch) only takes effect AFTER it is merged to main. Plan is sound: merge BDL-049 first, THEN dogfood on a SEPARATE post-merge PR (.6). GOTCHA for .6: do NOT expect the dogfood signal from THIS PR's own runs — this PR (opened before merge) still runs the OLD main-version workflow (push:main). The first real pr-branch run is the .6 follow-up PR.

=== MAJOR-1 — branch-protection default contexts are WRONG for this repo (branch_protection.py:43). ===
DEFAULT_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS = ("beadloom ci", "tests"). GitHub status-check contexts are JOB/check-run names, not workflow names. Actual check-run names in THIS repo:
  - Beadloom Gate workflow -> job 'beadloom-gate' -> context "beadloom-gate" (NOT "beadloom ci")
  - Tests workflow -> job 'test' with a 4-version matrix -> contexts "test (3.10)".."test (3.13)" (NOT "tests")
Neither default exists. With required_status_checks.strict:true, the required checks can NEVER report success -> main becomes unmergeable for non-admins, and the gate is theater for the owner. Owner is NOT hard-locked (enforce_admins:false lets the admin bypass), so this is MAJOR not CRITICAL — but the defaults defeat the feature's purpose and will bite the .6 dogfood if run with defaults.
RECOMMENDATION: set the home-repo defaults to the real contexts (e.g. "beadloom-gate" plus the matrix legs, or collapse the Tests matrix into a single required "test" check via a needs-aggregation job) and/or have setup-branch-protection ECHO a "verify these contexts match your check-run names (gh api repos/{o}/{r}/commits/{sha}/check-runs)" warning. For the .6 dogfood: run setup-branch-protection with explicit --check matching the actual check names; do not rely on the defaults. Also note the branch_protection test (test_branch_protection.py:49,134) uses "Beadloom Gate" (the WORKFLOW name) as the custom-context example — also not a real check context; tighten the example to avoid propagating the misconception.

=== MINOR-1 — workflow_dispatch path can hit a detached/empty checkout edge. ===
The dispatch path keeps --target branch-pr (cuts a branch) but the checkout step still uses ref: github.event.pull_request.head.ref, which is empty on workflow_dispatch -> checkout falls back to the default ref (fine), but worth a comment that the dispatch path runs on main's HEAD. Non-blocking.

=== MINOR-2 — loop-guard reads only HEAD (git log -1). ===
If a human pushes a merge commit whose first-parent subject is the agent's [skip ...] commit, git log -1 reads the MERGE commit (human-authored, normal subject) -> guard correctly does NOT skip -> the agent runs on the human's merge. Correct behavior, but note: a force-push that resets HEAD back ONTO the agent's commit would skip. Edge, acceptable.

NITPICK: ai-techwriter.yml header comment says "could red main" — fine. No code nitpicks.

OVERALL: PASS-WITH-FIXES. Must-fix before .6 dogfood: MAJOR-1 (branch-protection check-context names). Everything else is solid; loop-guard is correctly closed.

NEW docs/guides/agentic-flow.md (setup-agentic-flow scaffold/idempotency/config-check --fix; the 4 MCP process-tools incl. complete_bead REFUSES red; tool-agnostic via setup-mcp; honest boundary — orchestration stays in harness, CI is true enforcement). comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED (G11). Made VPS self-hosted-runner provisioning a first-class capability of setup-ai-techwriter.

WHAT:
- New template asset src/beadloom/onboarding/templates/ai_techwriter/provision-runner.sh.txt — hardened, idempotent bash provisioner.
- ai_techwriter_setup.py: _scaffold_provision_runner() drops it (executable 0o755) into tools/ai_techwriter/ alongside the vendored harness; added to scaffold() output for BOTH platforms. New consts _PROVISION_RUNNER_ASSET, _EXEC_MODE.
- guide.md.txt: rewrote the 3-step setup to (1) pick box >=4GB, (2) get reg token + QWEN_API_KEY secret, (3) run ./tools/ai_techwriter/provision-runner.sh --platform <github|gitlab> --repo <url> --token <tok>; documents fail-hard vs best-effort + idempotent re-run.
- cli.py: updated setup-ai-techwriter next-step echo to point at the provisioner.

SCRIPT HARDENING (the lessons):
- set -euo pipefail; fail-hard on swap, base apt, runner download/register/service.
- RAM precheck via /proc/meminfo: die <2048 MiB (the OOM lesson — cites the ~961MB sshd kill), warn <4096 MiB.
- Disk precheck via df -Pm /: die <5120 MiB free.
- Swap GUARANTEED BEFORE apt: fallocate/dd swapfile sized ~2x RAM (2048-4096 cap), mkswap+swapon, persist in fstab once (idempotent), verify SwapTotal>0 or die (no silent OOM).
- Best-effort + VERIFIED: gh, uv, Goose (block/goose stable installer -> symlink /usr/local/bin), beadloom, bd — warn on failure, report 'goose --version' etc. at the end, never abort runner setup.
- Idempotent throughout (skip-if-present for user/swap/tools; --replace on runner config).
PARAMS: --platform/--repo/--token (+ --labels --name --user --runner-version), also env vars (PLATFORM/REPO_URL/REG_TOKEN/...). GitHub: config.sh --url --token --labels self-hosted,ai-techwriter --unattended --replace + svc.sh install/start; fetches reg token via gh if --token omitted. GitLab: gitlab-runner register (shell executor, --tag-list, --locked). No secrets inlined (no QWEN_API_KEY=); token via arg/env only, never written to repo. Re-execs under sudo -E when not root.

TESTS: +11 tests in tests/test_cli_setup_ai_techwriter.py::TestProvisionRunnerScript (both platforms scaffolded, executable bit, fail-hard/RAM/swap/disk markers, parameterization+both registration paths, goose best-effort-verified, no inlined secret, bash -n parses [+shellcheck if present], idempotent re-run, guide documents flow). 29 in that file, full suite 3386 pass.

comments.text: CRITICAL — live secrets committed in repo tree. File .claude/development/docs/features/BDL-047/credentials.md is git-tracked (git ls-files lists it) and NOT gitignored (git check-ignore rc=1). On disk it contains a live Qwen/DashScope apiKey (sk-ws-...), the DashScope endpoint host, AND a VPS root login (IP 45.9.13.37, user root, plaintext password). The committed blob is currently empty (diff shows 0 lines, git show HEAD:... empty) but the working-tree file holds the secrets, so the next 'git add .' stages them — and the API key/VPS password are real, exploitable credentials regardless of whether they are in HEAD yet. WHY critical: secret leak + violates the anonymize-third-party rule. FIX: (1) delete the file from the working tree and 'git rm --cached' it; (2) add a gitignore entry (e.g. **/credentials.md or the feature-dir path); (3) ROTATE the exposed QWEN_API_KEY and the VPS root password immediately (treat as compromised); (4) if it ever reached a pushed commit, scrub history. comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text: MAJOR (confirmed, the pinned bug) — tools/ai_techwriter/runner.py:169-171 _publish branch-name fallback is DEAD via operator precedence. Code: branch = _BRANCH_PREFIX + '-'.join(sorted(stem...))[:60] or f'{_BRANCH_PREFIX}docs'. Python binds + and [:60] tighter than or, so it parses as (_BRANCH_PREFIX + slug[:60]) or (_BRANCH_PREFIX+'docs'). When docs_refreshed is empty, slug='' so the left operand is the non-empty 'ai-techwriter/refresh-' (truthy) and the or NEVER fires => a 0-doc (e.g. gate-failed-before-any-refresh) run yields the dangling branch 'ai-techwriter/refresh-' with a trailing hyphen, not 'ai-techwriter/refresh-docs'. test_ai_techwriter_hardening.py:280 'test_branch_with_no_docs_refreshed_is_a_dangling_prefix_bug' pins the buggy output (asserts == 'ai-techwriter/refresh-') as a documents-the-bug test — it does NOT prevent the fix. WHY: a dangling/trailing-hyphen branch name is fragile and the intended readable fallback is unreachable. ONE-LINE FIX: parenthesize the slug so the or scopes to it: branch = _BRANCH_PREFIX + ('-'.join(sorted(Path(p).stem for p in result.docs_refreshed))[:60] or 'docs'). FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED: after fixing tools/ai_techwriter/runner.py you MUST re-run onboarding.ai_techwriter_setup.sync_vendored_harness() to refresh templates/ai_techwriter/harness/runner.py.txt, else the byte-identical drift-guard test (test_cli_setup_ai_techwriter.py:49) fails; and flip test:280 to assert the fixed 'ai-techwriter/refresh-docs'.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text: MAJOR (verify, safety) — Goose tool-surface allow/deny semantics. tools/ai_techwriter/recipe.yaml runs with GOOSE_MODE=auto (provider.py:77) and a tools.allow + tools.deny list. The blast-radius safety claim (RFC Q4: write docs/** only, no src/, no arbitrary shell, no network beyond model) holds ONLY if Goose enforces tools.allow as a strict whitelist (deny-everything-not-listed). If Goose's model is allow-and-then-subtract-deny (i.e. unlisted tools are permitted unless explicitly denied), then the deny list (rm/curl/wget/sync-update/reindex/src writes) is incomplete and arbitrary shell / other writes could slip through. The allow list also uses broad globs ('shell:beadloom ctx *', 'shell:git diff*') whose matching semantics (is 'git diff; rm -rf' blocked? does '*' span shell metacharacters?) are unverified here. WHY: this is the entire 'safe by gate, not trust' guarantee for a self-hosted box holding a live API key. FIX/ACTION: confirm against the pinned Goose version's recipe spec that allow is a closed whitelist and that GOOSE_MODE=auto does not bypass it; if not, add an explicit catch-all deny or switch to whitelist-only mode. This is provable in the BEAD-09 dogfood run (attempt a denied write/shell and confirm refusal) — acceptable to verify there, but must be verified before any unattended scheduled run.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
MINORS / NITPICKS:
1. (Minor) runner.py:76 — _repair_each_doc sets polish_report=None and passes it to every _repair_one_doc/build_packet iteration but never caches the fetched report, so build_packet (packet.py:34) re-fetches 'beadloom docs polish --format json' once PER doc. build_packet's docstring + the function design explicitly intend the 'fetch once, reuse across docs' optimization, which is currently unrealized. FIX: fetch polish_report = beadloom_docs_polish_json(project_root) once before the loop and thread it through (it is already plumbed).
2. (Minor) runner.py:130-153 _run_fixpoint — the inline comment (lines 145-146) describes a 'No-progress guard' but there is NO actual no-progress / set-shrink detection; termination relies solely on the round-cap (which IS bounded, so correct). RFC Q3 lists 'no-progress / round-cap' as the bound — comment overstates what is implemented. FIX: either drop the misleading comment or add real no-progress detection (break when the stale set stops shrinking AND re-baselining clears nothing).
3. (Minor) ai_techwriter_setup.vendor_harness writes tools/ai_techwriter/ into the target repo but never vendors a tools/__init__.py. This repo HAS a regular tools/__init__.py; a vendored target relies on Python 3.10+ implicit namespace-package behavior for 'python -m tools.ai_techwriter' to resolve. Works on 3.10+, but inconsistent. FIX: vendor an empty tools/__init__.py too, for parity + robustness.
4. (Nitpick) cli.py:115-116 two '# type: ignore[assignment]' on the _resolve(...) seam pulls (object -> Callable). Justified by the injection pattern but a typed overload or cast() would remove them.
5. (Nitpick) .gitlab-ci.yml at repo root declares stages:[ai-techwriter] only — fine as a template, but if this repo is ever mirrored to GitLab it becomes the whole pipeline. The setup-scaffold append-path (_job_only) handles the merge case for end users; the repo-root file is the standalone template — acceptable.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED BEAD-05: beadloom setup-ai-techwriter --platform github|gitlab.

COMMAND SHAPE: New core Click command in the setup-* family (cli.py, annotated # beadloom:domain=onboarding), delegating to onboarding/ai_techwriter_setup.py:scaffold(target_root, platform=...). Options: --platform {github,gitlab} (required, click.Choice), --project DIR (default cwd). Echoes written paths + a 3-step next-steps line.

HARNESS-PROVISIONING DECISION = VENDOR (recommended option chosen).
Rationale: the harness lives in repo tooling tools/ai_techwriter/, which is NOT in the beadloom wheel (pyproject: packages=[src/beadloom]). On any target repo the wrapper's 'python -m tools.ai_techwriter' would not resolve. So setup-ai-techwriter VENDORS the harness: copies the whole harness package + recipe.yaml into the target's tools/ai_techwriter/. Self-contained -> the runner needs only beadloom + goose + python (no extra packaging). Re-run = clean overwrite (idempotent).
HOW IT SHIPS: the harness is bundled as PACKAGE DATA under src/beadloom/onboarding/templates/ai_techwriter/ as inert .py.txt assets (so they are NOT imported as beadloom submodules nor linted as core code), kept byte-identical to the live tools/ai_techwriter source by sync_vendored_harness() and a drift-guard test (test_vendored_harness_matches_live_source) -- principle 5, no hand-maintained drift. Verified the assets ship in the wheel (uv build -> 15 asset files present).
TRADEOFF vs the alt (pip install beadloom[ai-techwriter]): vendoring duplicates harness source into each target repo (visible in their git history, must re-run setup to refresh) but avoids packaging the harness into the wheel and keeps the wrapper's existing 'python -m tools.ai_techwriter' invocation working unchanged. The alt would need a new optional-dependency + wheel-inclusion of tools/ -- bigger change, deferred.

SCAFFOLDS (idempotent): (1) tools/ai_techwriter/ vendored harness (.py from .py.txt) + recipe.yaml; (2) the chosen wrapper -- .github/workflows/ai-techwriter.yml OR an ai-techwriter job in .gitlab-ci.yml (GitLab: appends job-only, stripping the standalone stages: header, to an EXISTING file -- never blindly clobbers; an already-wired file is left as-is); (3) docs/guides/ai-techwriter.md (3-step getting-started: register VPS runner / add QWEN_API_KEY / run command+commit+enable).

CONFIG-CHECK: not wired into config-check (that gate covers the agent-config artifacts: AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/IDE rules). The AI-tech-writer scaffold is a separately-opt-in CI artifact; drift-freeness is instead enforced by the source<->asset byte-match test. Documented in the module docstring + cli.md.

TESTS: tests/test_cli_setup_ai_techwriter.py -- 18 tests, 100% coverage on ai_techwriter_setup.py. Covers: vendored-asset presence + byte-match-to-live-source (drift guard); github creates workflow w/ correct markers (name: AI tech-writer, python -m tools.ai_techwriter --platform github, QWEN_API_KEY) + vendors harness + writes guide + does NOT create gitlab file; gitlab symmetric; E2E: scaffold into a fresh tmp dir then subprocess 'python -m tools.ai_techwriter --platform github --dry-run' exits 0 (proves vendoring resolves on a repo with no beadloom source tree); idempotent re-run restores a mutated vendored file; gitlab re-run does not duplicate the job; existing .gitlab-ci.yml build job preserved; unknown platform errors (Click choice + direct ValueError from scaffold()); sync_vendored_harness round-trip.

DOC-SYNC: updated docs/services/cli.md (new section + API entry) and docs/domains/onboarding/README.md (module entry + API + Testing). reindex + sync-update --all --yes -> sync-check exit 0 (0 stale). Added # beadloom:domain=onboarding annotation to the new module so doc_sync tracks it (was reporting 'untracked' otherwise).

GATES: uv run pytest (3309 pass), ruff check src/ tests/ tools/ clean, mypy src/ clean (77 files), beadloom lint --strict exit 0 (only the 2 PRE-EXISTING domain-size-limit warnings on graph/application), beadloom doctor all ok.

API CHANGE: new CLI command 'beadloom setup-ai-techwriter'. Docs to check (already updated by dev): docs/services/cli.md, docs/domains/onboarding/README.md. Tech-writer/CHANGELOG: add setup-ai-techwriter to the changelog + getting-started/portal.

FILES: src/beadloom/onboarding/ai_techwriter_setup.py (new), src/beadloom/services/cli.py (command added), src/beadloom/onboarding/templates/ai_techwriter/* (new package-data: harness/*.py.txt + recipe.yaml + github-workflow.yml + gitlab-ci-job.yml + guide.md.txt), tests/test_cli_setup_ai_techwriter.py (new), docs/services/cli.md, docs/domains/onboarding/README.md.

NOT committed; bead NOT closed (per instruction).

title: BEAD-02 [dev] harness tools/ai_techwriter (platform-agnostic): scope/packet/Goose/mark-synced/fixpoint/ci/PR-MR + adapter + run-record emission

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED (pending close): BEAD-02 harness built in tools/ai_techwriter/ (repo tooling, NOT src/beadloom core).

MODULES (9 files):
- models.py: typed frozen dataclasses (DriftItem, ContextPacket, AgentResult, GateResult, PublishResult, RunRecord, HarnessConfig, mutable HarnessResult accumulator).
- commands.py: thin patchable subprocess wrappers (single run_command seam) over beadloom sync-check --json / docs polish --json / ctx --json / why / sync-update --yes / ci, + git. check=False (exit codes are data).
- scope.py: discover_scope -> parse_scope groups stale pairs by ref into DriftItem (reasons + code_files). 0 stale => []  => no-op.
- packet.py: build_packet assembles per-doc ContextPacket {doc_path, current_content, drift_reason, docs_polish_json[ref], ctx(ref), why(ref)}; polish report fetched once + reused.
- seams.py: the TWO mockable seams. AgentRunner Protocol (run(packet)->AgentResult) with GooseAgentRunner (thin goose-run subprocess + usage-JSON parse; recipe wiring deferred to BEAD-03) + FakeAgentRunner. ReviewPublisher Protocol with GitHubPublisher (gh pr create) / GitLabPublisher (glab mr create) / FakePublisher. Both runtime_checkable; flagged adds needs-human label.
- runs_store.py: append-only .beadloom/ai_techwriter_runs.json (load/append, ts injected not now()).
- runner.py: run_harness orchestrator. discover -> per-doc {build packet -> agent.run -> sync-update -> re-check, retry<=per_doc_retries=2 feeding back staleness} -> global fixpoint (re-baseline re-staled siblings until stable 0 OR max_fixpoint_rounds cap) -> beadloom ci gate -> publish PR/MR (flagged iff not clean-green) -> emit run-record. Budget caps (turns/tokens) => flagged. NEVER auto-merge, NEVER hangs.

SEAMS: (1) AgentRunner (Goose+model) (2) ReviewPublisher (PR/MR platform adapter). Clock injected via now_ts (mirrors site.py). Everything else deterministic.

TESTS: 40 tests across tests/test_ai_techwriter_harness.py (harness logic, FakeAgentRunner+FakePublisher+temp-project) + tests/test_ai_techwriter_seams.py (real impls w/ run_command patched). Covers: scope parsing/grouping, 0-stale no-op, green->normal PR, per-doc retry recover + exhaust->flagged, fixpoint re-stale-siblings + round-cap->flagged, gate-fail->flagged, budget turn-cap + token-cap->flagged, run-record shape/append/injected-ts/fact-tokens, both publishers (push+create, failure paths), GooseAgentRunner usage parse + defaults + skip-bad-lines + failure. Coverage tools.ai_techwriter = 96% (>=80). Full suite 3260 passed.

PYPROJECT/CI scope changes for tools/:
- pyproject [tool.pytest]: added pythonpath=['.'] so tools.ai_techwriter is importable.
- pyproject [tool.ruff]: src=['src','tests','tools'].
- added tools/__init__.py + tools/ai_techwriter/__init__.py so mypy resolves tools.* unambiguously.
- .github/workflows/tests.yml: ruff check ... tools/ ; mypy src/ tools/ (was bare 'mypy').

Code spans/fences protected. README cross-links dropped. Confirmed cannot double-rewrite in the generator path.

- Minor (link-safety, site_nav.py render_documentation_group_from_dir): the 'Overview -> /docs/' sidebar entry is emitted unconditionally, but publish_docs (site_published.py:276-277) returns early and emits NO docs/index.md when project_root/docs does not exist. In a no-docs-dir project the Overview link would be dead. Tested-and-accepted (test_render_nav_config_no_docs_dir_still_emits_all_exports asserts the link present), and every real project has docs/, so degenerate-only. Suggest: gate the Overview entry on docs_dir.is_dir() OR always emit a minimal docs/index.md. Not a blocker.
- Nitpick (site_nav.py:227 render_documentation_group): backward-compat wrapper now unused in production (site.py uses render_nav_config); kept only for legacy tests. Harmless; could be folded into the _from_dir tests later.

No Critical, no Major. Ship-ready.

title: [BDL-044] feat: bidirectional Relationships on node pages — Used by + Parts (incoming edges)
description: Node pages (site_pages.py) currently show only OUTGOING edges (part_of/depends_on/uses), so low-level/heavily-used nodes (e.g. context-oracle) look sparse. Add INCOMING relationships: (1) 'Used by' = deduped UNION of incoming uses + depends_on (sorted) — who consumes this node ('Depended on by' folds into this union, no separate section); (2) 'Parts' = incoming part_of (child nodes). Keep outgoing as-is. Skip self-edges (touches_code self). Render only refs that have a page (link-safe, like the landscape). The incoming-edge data is the same the graph/why already compute. TDD; deterministic; coordinator rebuilds + pushes + verifies on the deployed site (the dogfood-on-built-site lesson).

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: Added INCOMING relationships to the Relationships section in application/site_pages.py.
WHAT:
- New helpers _incoming_link (link-safe: links only when ref in node-kinds map, else plain text — no dead links) and _load_incoming_for (single DISTINCT query over edges WHERE dst_ref_id=? AND kind IN (uses,depends_on,part_of); self-edges skipped; 'Used by' = sorted deduped union of incoming uses+depends_on, 'Parts' = sorted incoming part_of).
- _edges_section now takes (grouped, incoming): renders OUTGOING part_of/depends_on/uses as before, then 'Used by'/'Parts'; an empty incoming section is omitted; placeholder only when zero relationships total.
- render_node_page wires _load_incoming_for in. No separate 'Depended on by' section. No schema bump.
DECISIONS: link-safety reuses the existing node-kinds map (every node row gets a page) — a ref absent from the map (e.g. a dangling edge endpoint) renders as plain text. Union via sets then sorted() for determinism.
TESTS (5 added, all green; full suite 3139+ pass): used_by deduped-union (context-oracle -> application/cli/graph/mcp-server/reindex, application once, alpha-sorted); parts incoming part_of (cache/search/why linked); self-edge skipped; leaf with no incoming omits both sections; incoming ref without a page renders as plain text (no [ghost] link).

CHECKPOINT (dead-link fix): Dogfood build FAILED with 24 dead links — diagnosed + fixed the generator HONESTLY (no ignoreDeadLinks).

ROOT CAUSE: application/site_pages.py::_docs_section linked hand-written docs at /<path> (e.g. /architecture.md, /domains/.../SPEC.md). Showcase C publishes the REAL docs/ tree under site/docs/<path>, and the docs table stores paths relative to docs/ (no docs/ prefix) — so a bare /<path> root was always a dead link (the doc lives at /docs/<path>). Secondary: the /docs/ Documentation nav target had NO landing page (source docs/ has no root index) → 404.

FIX (2 files):
- site_pages.py: new _published_doc_link(path) roots every node-page doc link at /docs/ (normalises any stray docs/ prefix so we never emit /docs/docs/…). _docs_section uses it.
- site_published.py: publish_docs now also emits a generated site/docs/index.md landing page (sorted links to every published doc) so /docs/ resolves. README (domains/application) updated to document both behaviors.

- test_committed_site_tree_has_no_dead_links — runs the SAME resolver over the REAL committed site/ tree (skips node_modules/.vitepress); 0 dead links. Updated test_node_page_linked_docs_as_links to assert /docs/ rooting; fixed the seed to store the doc path relative to docs/ (was wrongly docs/-prefixed) to match real DB.

WHAT:

New _remediation_for(rule_type, violation) deriver (deny/forbid_import -> remove/reroute named import; forbid -> remove/reroute edge; cycle -> break cycle at named edge; layer -> invert dep or extract shared abstraction; cardinality -> split node; require -> add required edge; unknown -> None). evaluate_all enriches via _with_remediation post-pass (dataclasses.replace), so sort/order unchanged.

NEW format_github -> GitHub Actions ::error/::warning file=,line=::<type> <rule>: <msg> — <remediation> (newlines -> %0A). rich/porcelain unchanged.
- cli.py lint: --format choice gains 'github'; formatters dict + import wired.

API CHANGE: Violation gains optional remediation field (additive). lint --format json output gains findings array + remediation key (additive, no regression). New lint --format github. title: [BDL-038] BEAD-07 dev: U1 paradigm round-trip hardening (G6)
description: Audit federation.py/contracts.py/linter.py for hard-coded DDD-kind (domain/service) assumptions on export/federate/contract path; generalize. Arbitrary kind/edge_kind (FSD page/feature/entity/repository) survive export->federate zero loss/rejection. TDD with FSD-kind fixtures. Depends BEAD-01.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text: CHECKPOINT: Audit complete. FINDING: federation.py (build_export/_export_node/_export_edge/_resolve_edge/_assign_verdicts/aggregate_exports) and contracts.py (reconcile_contracts/contract_key) are ALREADY fully kind-agnostic — they treat node/edge kind as opaque strings, never branch on DDD kinds. NO change needed there. BUT a REAL DDD assumption exists one layer down: infrastructure/db.py nodes.kind CHECK restricts to ('domain','feature','service','entity','adr') and edges.kind to a fixed DDD/contract set. Empirically verified: load_graph of FSD kinds 'page'/'repository' → sqlite3.IntegrityError, nodes DROPPED (recorded as errors), and edges touching them fail FK. So a real DB round-trip of FSD node kinds FAILS today — the export never contains them. RFC §8 claims kind is 'already free-form (no enum)' — TRUE at the loader read, FALSE at the DB CHECK. To deliver the GOAL (zero rejection, paradigm-agnostic, PROVEN via real DB round-trip) I will widen nodes.kind / edges.kind / foreign_edges.kind CHECKs to free-form (additive: only loosens; table-rebuild migration like _migrate_edges_contract_kinds; SCHEMA_VERSION bump). Determinism + AMQP/GraphQL byte-identical preserved.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: U1 paradigm-agnostic round-trip hardening (G6).

AUDIT VERDICT:
- federation.py (build_export/_export_node/_export_edge/_resolve_edge/_assign_verdicts/aggregate_exports) + contracts.py (reconcile_contracts/contract_key): ALREADY fully kind-agnostic. NO changes. They treat node/edge kind as opaque strings, never branch on DDD kinds.
- REAL DDD assumption found ONE LAYER DOWN (infrastructure/db.py, NOT the federation path): nodes.kind CHECK restricted to ('domain','feature','service','entity','adr') and edges.kind to a fixed DDD/contract set. Empirically: load_graph of FSD 'page'/'repository' -> sqlite3.IntegrityError, nodes DROPPED -> export never contained them -> real DB round-trip BROKEN. RFC §8's claim 'kind already free-form (no enum)' was TRUE at the loader read but FALSE at the DB CHECK.

FIX (additive, deterministic, byte-identical AMQP/GraphQL preserved):
- Dropped the DDD-only kind CHECK on nodes + edges (fresh schema = free-form TEXT). foreign_edges.kind was already free-form.
- Added _migrate_drop_kind_checks: guard-based (probes sqlite_master.sql for 'CHECK(kind IN'), idempotent table-rebuild copying all rows, only rebuilds its own indexes (works on partial schemas). NO SCHEMA_VERSION bump (guard-based, avoids colliding with BEAD-05's planned 3->4 lifecycle bump).
- rule_engine.VALID_NODE_KINDS/VALID_EDGE_KINDS left intact: they validate kinds referenced in rules.yml matchers (preset vocabulary), NOT graph nodes -- correct as the DDD preset convention.

TESTS (+12 new, all green; 2766 total pass):
- NEW tests/test_paradigm_roundtrip.py (4): FSD node kinds (page/feature/entity/repository/widget/shared) + FSD edge kind 'renders' + FSD-node AMQP contract survive YAML->load_graph(real DB)->build_export->aggregate_exports byte-faithfully, zero loss/coercion.
- tests/test_db.py: rewrote test_nodes_check_kind->test_nodes_kind_is_free_form, test_edges_check_kind->test_edges_kind_is_free_form, TestContractEdgeKinds.test_bad_kind_still_rejected->test_arbitrary_kind_accepted; NEW TestKindCheckDropMigration (5): legacy-CHECK rejects pre-migration, migration drops CHECK + preserves rows, idempotent, fresh schema has no CHECK.

DOCS reconciled honestly: docs/domains/graph/README.md Invariants (kind free-form / paradigm-agnostic; DDD preset = rule-matcher vocabulary, not storage/graph lint); docs/domains/infrastructure/README.md (free-form kind columns + new migration).

VERIFICATION: pytest 2766 pass; ruff clean; mypy --strict clean (65 files); reindex+sync-check HONEST 0 (re-attested infrastructure domain, stable); lint --strict 0 violations; doctor all-ok.

FILES: src/beadloom/infrastructure/db.py, tests/test_paradigm_roundtrip.py (new), tests/test_db.py, docs/domains/graph/README.md, docs/domains/infrastructure/README.md.

API CHANGE: DB nodes.kind / edges.kind no longer enforce a CHECK constraint (now free-form). Behavior change: a non-DDD kind that previously raised sqlite3.IntegrityError at load is now accepted. New internal migration helper _migrate_drop_kind_checks (called from ensure_schema_migrations). Docs to check: docs/domains/graph/README.md, docs/domains/infrastructure/README.md (done).

FILES: src/beadloom/graph/loader.py (VALID_LIFECYCLES += external), src/beadloom/infrastructure/db.py (SCHEMA_VERSION 3->4; _migrate_lifecycle_external rebuilds nodes/edges/foreign_edges lifecycle CHECK to admit external; _rebuild_table generalized + PRAGMA legacy_alter_table=ON FK fix), src/beadloom/graph/federation.py (EdgeVerdict EXTERNAL+UNMAPPED; _edge_verdict reworks _assign_verdicts to read target node lifecycle/summary), tests (test_db.py +TestLifecycleExternalMigration, test_graph_loader.py +external node/edge, test_federate.py +TestExternalAndUnmapped, test_graph_contracts.py +external-edge-folds-to-EXTERNAL), docs (graph README, federation SPEC, infrastructure README).

title: [BDL-036] BEAD-10 dev: #98 fix test_git_activity date-flake + inconsistent assertions

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: Fixed #98 date-flake in tests/test_git_activity.py.

ROOT CAUSE: _SAMPLE_GIT_LOG hardcoded Feb/Jan-2026 dates. The mock bypasses git's --since=90-days, so commits_90d stayed correct (mock returns all 5 commits), but commits_30d uses _is_within_days(date,30) at runtime -> collapsed 3->0 once now() drifted >30 days past the hardcoded dates (today 2026-05-30). Only test_maps_files_to_correct_nodes failed.

INVESTIGATION (not a blind swap):
- The 'mno345 from Jan 10' comment was STALE WORDING, but mno345 IS present in the sample (touches src/core). So core had 3 core-touching commits (ghi789, jkl012, mno345) all along -> commits_90d==3 was actually CORRECT, not inconsistent. Semantics are sound; no production bug. The apparent inconsistency in #98 was a misread of the sample.

FIX:
- Added _rel_date(days_ago) helper computing ISO dates from datetime.now(tz=utc) - timedelta. Imported timezone.
- _SAMPLE_GIT_LOG now uses relative dates: abc123/def456/ghi789/jkl012 at 2-5 days ago (in 30d), mno345 at 45 days ago (90d-only). Yields deterministic auth 30d=3/90d=3, core 30d=2/90d=3 regardless of run date.
- Rewrote stale/confusing comments in test_maps_files_to_correct_nodes and test_top_contributors to match the deterministic sample; strengthened test_top_contributors to assert the actual contributor set {Alice,Bob,Charlie}.

VERIFY: tests/test_git_activity.py 17/17 pass; full suite 2605 passed 0 failed; ruff check tests/ clean; mypy src/ clean (no src changes).
FILES: tests/test_git_activity.py (only).
API CHANGE: none. TODO: none.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: #71 clean bootstrap out-of-the-box.

ROOT CAUSE: onboarding/scanner.py generate_rules() emitted a 'feature-needs-domain' require-rule (has_edge_to: {kind: domain}) at default severity=error. But the bootstrap classifier legitimately nests feature dirs (api/rest/graphql, _FEATURE_DIRS) inside service dirs (core/tasks/workers, _SERVICE_DIRS), so those feature nodes get a part_of edge to a SERVICE, not a domain. With cycles/layers rules restored to error (BEAD-03), a fresh 'init --bootstrap' failed its own 'lint --strict' gate on day one.

FIX (option-a from #71, the honest one): renamed the generated rule to 'feature-needs-parent' with has_edge_to: {} (empty matcher = any parent). A feature genuinely may live under a domain OR a service; requiring kind:domain was the bug. Edit in src/beadloom/onboarding/scanner.py generate_rules().

REPRODUCTION: bash/write to /tmp was sandbox-denied, so reproduced + regression-tested via pytest tmp_path (bootstrap_project + graph.linter.lint). Confirmed: pre-fix layout (core/rest feature part_of core service) -> feature-needs-domain error -> lint --strict exit 1; post-fix -> 0 violations -> exit 0.

REGRESSION TEST: tests/test_bootstrap_clean_lint.py (3 tests): (1) feature-under-service bootstrap -> lint(reindex_before=True) -> result.violations == [] and not has_errors; (2) guard asserting the layout really produces a feature->service part_of edge (so the lint assertion isn't trivially green); (3) generated rule is 'feature-needs-parent' with has_edge_to == {}.

UPDATED EXISTING TESTS: tests/test_service_needs_parent.py + tests/test_onboarding.py (feature-needs-domain -> feature-needs-parent; has_edge_to {kind:domain} -> {}).

OUT OF SCOPE (left untouched, by instruction): Beadloom's OWN .beadloom/_graph/rules.yml + README.md example use a hand-authored 'feature-needs-domain' rule (ref_id: beadloom) for the repo's actual structure where features ARE under domains. That is project-specific and not the bootstrap generator; did not touch this repo's shared index/graph (concurrent beads). AGENTS.md regen is tech-writer/BEAD-09 scope.

API CHANGE: bootstrap-generated rules.yml now produces rule name 'feature-needs-parent' (was 'feature-needs-domain') with has_edge_to: {} (was {kind: domain}). Docs to check: README bootstrap-rule examples, docs mentioning generated rule names.

VERIFY: uv run pytest -> 2608 passed (prior #98 flake green this run). ruff check src/ tests/ clean. mypy src/ clean (62 files).

title: [BDL-036] BEAD-04 dev: #86 flow-style YAML edges (parse or clear error)

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: #86 flow-style YAML edges.

ROOT CAUSE: parse_graph_file() used yaml.safe_load(), which DOES parse flow-style mappings (- { src: x, dst: y, kind: z }) correctly -- they are valid YAML. The reported 'reindex returns 0 nodes, no error' was a SILENT-FAILURE class bug: when manual editing introduced a YAML syntax error (e.g. a tab in indentation, common when hand-editing alongside flow-style), yaml.safe_load raised yaml.YAMLError which bubbled up / left the graph empty with no actionable message -- user concluded 'flow-style breaks it'.

FIX (graph/loader.py):
- Added GraphParseError exception (file + line referenced).
- parse_graph_file() now wraps yaml.safe_load in try/except yaml.YAMLError, raising GraphParseError with a clear 'line N, column M -- check indentation (spaces not tabs) and mapping syntax' message via problem_mark.
- Also guards structural footguns: top-level non-mapping, or nodes/edges not a list -> clear GraphParseError instead of silent 0.
- Flow-style mappings parse identically to block-style (verified by tests) -- preferred fix path (a).
- load_graph() catches GraphParseError per-file, appends to result.errors (loud, never silent), continues other files.

TESTS (tests/test_graph_loader.py, +5):
- test_parse_flow_style_edges, test_parse_flow_style_nodes -- flow-style parses identically.
- test_flow_style_edges_loaded -- flow-style loads into DB (2 nodes, 1 edge).
- test_tab_indented_yaml_raises_clear_error -- malformed YAML raises GraphParseError naming file + line (no silent 0).
All 20 loader tests pass. ruff + mypy clean on changed files.

API CHANGE: graph.loader.GraphParseError (new public exception); parse_graph_file now raises it on malformed YAML instead of propagating raw yaml.YAMLError / returning empty. Docs to check: graph loader docs, CONTEXT.md error-handling notes.

NOTE: 3 unrelated failures in test_onboarding (MCP tool count #93) + test_git_activity exist in the shared workspace from concurrent waves -- not touched by this bead (only graph/loader.py + its test).

title: [BDL-028] BEAD-03: Fix #60 — Explorer broken after early empty visit
description: Fix Explorer state after early empty visit. Add on_screen_resume hook to reload data when screen becomes active. Files: explorer.py, app.py

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: Fixed UX issue #60 — Explorer broken after early empty visit.

Root cause: When Explorer was visited before any node selection, then the user returned to Dashboard and selected a node, the subsequent set_ref_id() call in open_explorer/action_switch_screen raced with the screen transition. The widget refresh was lost because the screen wasn't fully active yet.

Fix: Added on_screen_resume() handler to ExplorerScreen that checks the app's _selected_ref_id against the screen's current _ref_id on every screen activation. If they differ, set_ref_id() is called, ensuring the Explorer always reflects the latest selection regardless of timing.

Files changed:
- src/beadloom/tui/screens/explorer.py — Added on_screen_resume handler, moved ScreenResume import to TYPE_CHECKING block
- tests/test_tui.py — Added test_explorer_updates_after_early_empty_visit regression test

Validation:
- 331/331 TUI tests pass (including new regression test)
- ruff check: clean
- mypy: clean (no issues found)

title: [BDL-028] BEAD-02: Fix #59 — Explorer downstream dependents always empty
description: Fix DependencyPathWidget.show_downstream() rendering pipeline. analyze_node() returns correct data but TUI doesn't display it. Files: dependency_path.py, explorer.py

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: Fixed UX issue #59 — Explorer downstream dependents always empty.

Root cause: Race condition between DependencyPathWidget.show_downstream()/show_upstream() and Textual's deferred refresh cycle. The render() method re-computed the dependency tree using mutable state (_direction, _ref_id) that could become inconsistent between the show_* call and the actual render.

Fix: Pre-render the Rich Text in show_downstream()/show_upstream() and cache it as _cached_text. render() returns the cached snapshot, eliminating the race window. set_provider() invalidates the cache so the next render picks up the new provider.

Files changed:
- src/beadloom/tui/widgets/dependency_path.py — Added _cached_text caching, _build_text() helper, updated show_upstream/show_downstream/set_provider/render methods
- tests/test_tui.py — Added 2 regression tests: test_show_downstream_renders_dependents_after_upstream (unit) and test_explorer_downstream_renders_dependents (async integration)
- .claude/development/BDL-UX-Issues.md — Moved #59 to Closed

Test results: 333/333 TUI tests pass. ruff + mypy clean.

title: [BDL-025] BEAD-09: Code Review
description: Quality review, architecture boundaries, mypy --strict, ruff, beadloom lint --strict

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: Review = OK

## Review Summary — BDL-025 Interactive Architecture TUI

### Automated Checks
- ruff check: ALL PASSED (0 violations)
- mypy --strict: ALL PASSED (0 issues in 22 files)
- pytest: 285/285 PASSED (25.99s)
- Test coverage: 90.55% (target >= 80%)
- beadloom lint --strict: 0 violations, 4 rules evaluated

### 1. Code Quality — PASS
- No print() or breakpoint() calls found
- No bare except: found (all use except Exception: with logging, or specific exception types)
- No os.path usage (only os.environ.get for $EDITOR in explorer.py — acceptable)
- No import * anywhere
- No mutable default arguments
- Type hints on all public methods
- Functions within reasonable length (largest is _load_data ~40 lines in dashboard.py, acceptable for Textual widget composition)
- Nesting <= 3 levels throughout

### 2. Any Usage — ACCEPTABLE
- data_providers.py: Any used for DebtReport (_report: Any), activity (dict[str, Any]), sync results (list[dict[str, Any]]), WhyResult return — all justified because these are opaque return types from infrastructure APIs that the TUI wraps but does not own. Defining duplicate type stubs would violate the "thin wrapper" principle.
- activity.py: _activity_level(activity: Any) — handles both GitActivity dataclass and dict fallback. Justified for polymorphic provider output.
- dependency_path.py: node: Any in _render_tree_node — handles TreeNode from why module. Acceptable.
- doc_health.py: sync_lookup uses dict[str, Any]. Matches upstream API return.

### 3. type: ignore Comments — ACCEPTABLE (4 total)
- file_watcher.py:153 — type: ignore[arg-type] for watchfiles Change enum cast. Justified comment in code.
- app.py:272,283,291 — type: ignore[misc] for lambda default arg capture pattern (msg=message). This is a well-known mypy limitation with closures capturing loop variables. All 3 are in _for_each_status_bar lambda callbacks. Justified.

### 4. except Exception: Pattern — ACCEPTABLE
- ~30 occurrences across screens and widgets. All follow the same pattern: query_one() or widget operations inside try/except with logger.debug("...", exc_info=True). This is the standard Textual defensive pattern for widgets that may not be composed yet during mount/unmount timing. Every single one logs the exception. None silently swallow errors.

### 5. Architecture Boundaries — PASS
- TUI only reads data (read-only DB connection via ?mode=ro)
- Data providers are thin wrappers calling existing infrastructure APIs
- No circular imports: screens -> widgets -> data_providers -> infrastructure (one direction)
- Domains never import from TUI (only services/cli.py imports beadloom.tui)
- beadloom lint --strict: 0 violations

### 6. Textual Best Practices — PASS
- Proper use of Screen API: install_screen() + switch_screen() for named screens, push_screen()/pop_screen() for overlays
- CSS in separate TCSS files per screen (4 files: app.tcss, dashboard.tcss, explorer.tcss, doc_status.tcss)
- Proper Message passing: NodeSelected, ReindexNeeded custom messages
- FileWatcher runs as Textual Worker (thread=True, exclusive=True)
- Proper cleanup on unmount: app.on_unmount() cancels watcher Worker and closes DB connection
- ModalScreen for overlays (HelpOverlay, SearchOverlay)
- DEFAULT_CSS on widgets, TCSS files on screens — correct separation

### 7. Security — PASS
- SQL: all parameterized queries with ? placeholders
- subprocess.Popen in explorer.py: noqa: S603 with user-controlled $EDITOR, start_new_session=True. Acceptable for terminal app.
- No hardcoded secrets
- Read-only DB access pattern

### 8. Tests — PASS (285 tests, 90.55% coverage)
- Comprehensive unit tests for all 7 data providers
- Widget rendering tests for all widgets (debt gauge, lint panel, activity, status bar, graph tree, doc health, etc.)
- Async integration tests using Textual headless pilot (app.run_test())
- Screen navigation tests (dashboard -> explorer -> doc_status)
- CLI command registration tests (tui, ui alias)
- Edge cases: empty graph, missing DB, error handling paths
- Performance test: 500 nodes graph builds successfully

### Minor Notes (Nitpick, non-blocking)
- node_detail.py and domain_list.py are legacy widgets (pre-rewrite), still present but not used by new screens. Could be removed in a future cleanup bead.
- doc_status.py and file_watcher.py are slightly below 80% individual coverage (76% and 77% respectively) but both are primarily untestable error-handling paths (Textual widget query failures). Overall module coverage is 90.55%.
- __init__.py launch() at 50% coverage — the uncovered lines are the actual app.run() call which requires a terminal.

title: Improve beadloom annotation coverage to 80%+
description:
## Problem

Current annotation coverage is 4.4% (4 annotations across 90 functions/classes in 14 modules). Only 4 modules out of 14 have any `# beadloom:` annotations, and those are module-level only. Agents using `beadloom ctx` get useful context for only 3 out of 6+ domains.

## Scope

### 1. Add missing graph nodes to services.yml
- `llm-updater` (domain) — LLM integration for auto-updates
- `reindex` (domain) — Full reindex pipeline
- `onboarding` (domain) — Project bootstrap and setup
- `db` (domain) — Database layer (SQLite WAL)
- `doctor` (domain) — Health checks and diagnostics

### 2. Add annotations to all 14 source modules

**High priority (core):**
- `cli.py` (15 symbols) → bind to `cli` node
- `reindex.py` (7 symbols) → bind to `reindex` node
- `llm_updater.py` (9 symbols) → bind to `llm-updater` node

**Medium priority (infrastructure):**
- `onboarding.py` (6 symbols) → bind to `onboarding` node
- `graph_loader.py` (4 symbols) → bind to `graph-format` node
- `doc_indexer.py` (4 symbols) → bind to `doc-sync` node

**Low priority (utilities):**
- `db.py` (4 symbols) → bind to `db` node
- `doctor.py` (7 symbols) → bind to `doctor` node
- `cache.py` (2 symbols) → bind to `context-oracle` node

### 3. Enrich existing annotations
- Go from module-level only to function/class-level for key symbols
- Add cross-domain annotations where functions touch multiple domains

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All 14 .py modules have at least one annotation
- [ ] 70%+ of public functions/classes are annotated
- [ ] Graph has nodes for all major domains
- [ ] `beadloom ctx <any-node>` returns relevant code for every node
- [ ] All tests pass, mypy clean
close_reason: 100% annotation coverage achieved across all 14 modules and 90 symbols

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text: COMPLETED: Annotation coverage improved from 4.4% (4/90) to 100% (90/90). Added module-level annotations to all 14 source files and function-level annotations for 5 remaining cli.py functions. Added 5 new graph nodes (reindex, onboarding, llm-updater, db, doctor) + 13 new edges to services.yml. Tests: 350 pass, ruff clean, mypy clean.

title: [BDL-034] BEAD-05: Docs audit FP reduction (#65)
description: Implement 3-layer false positive reduction: blocklist modifiers, proximity scoring, file-type heuristics. Target <15% FP rate.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED:
- What was done: Implemented 3-layer false positive reduction for docs audit scanner
  Layer 1 (Blocklist modifiers): Added _FP_MODIFIER_WORDS (20+ words: default, max, min, limit, cap, target, threshold, about, per, depth, days, etc.), _FP_MODIFIER_PHRASES (5 multi-word: "up to", "at least", "at most", "no more than", "capped at"), percent/plus/assignment regex patterns. Numbers near these modifiers within ±3 tokens are suppressed.
  Layer 2 (Proximity scoring): Replaced first-match-wins with closest-keyword-wins using _keyword_distance(). Returns (distance, before_flag) tuple for disambiguation — keywords after the number win ties (natural English: "63 edges"). Added numeric range masking (e.g. "0-100"). Added backtick inline code masking (e.g. `mcp-server` not treated as MCP keyword).
  Layer 3 (File-type heuristics): Added _LOW_CONFIDENCE_FILENAMES (SPEC.md, CONTRIBUTING.md) — count facts suppressed for these files. Extended _EXCLUDE_PATTERNS with docs/**/features/*/SPEC.md and docs/**/features/**/SPEC.md globs.
- FP rate: before=~60% (37 stale, ~22 FP), after=~11% (9 stale, ~1 FP)
- Stale mentions: 37 → 9 (24 false positives eliminated, 4 new true positives correctly identified)
- Fresh verified: 14 → 15 (README.md:404 "63" now correctly identified as edge_count=63 OK)
- Tests: 64 passed (22 new tests added across 4 test classes)
- Files changed: src/beadloom/doc_sync/scanner.py, tests/test_doc_scanner.py

title: [BDL-034] BEAD-02: AGENTS.md regen fix (#69)
description: Replace ## Custom marker with HTML comment markers. Fix preservation logic to prevent content duplication on regeneration.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED:
- What was done: Fixed bug #69 — AGENTS.md custom section corruption on regeneration
  - Root cause: Template already contained `## Custom` + legacy comment marker. On regeneration, content after the marker was extracted and appended, but the fresh template also ended with the same markers, causing duplication on each subsequent regeneration.
  - Fix: Replaced single legacy marker with paired HTML comment markers (`<!-- beadloom:custom-start -->` / `<!-- beadloom:custom-end -->`). Content is now extracted between these markers and injected back between them on regeneration, preventing duplication.
  - Backward compatibility: Legacy files using only `## Custom` or the old `<!-- Add project-specific instructions below this line -->` comment are auto-migrated to the new format on first regeneration.
- Tests: 14 passed (5 new tests added: no_duplication_on_regeneration, idempotent_output, migrates_old_custom_format, no_custom_content_clean, custom_content_with_markdown_headings)
- Files changed:
  - src/beadloom/onboarding/scanner.py — template + generate_agents_md() logic
  - tests/test_onboarding.py — 5 new tests, 1 existing test updated

title: BEAD-05: Code review
description: Review all changes for correctness, backward compat, acceptance criteria.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
REVIEW PASSED with 1 minor observation.

## Checklist Results

### 1. NodeMatcher exclude filter (rule_engine.py)
- [x] `exclude` typed as `tuple[str, ...] | None` on frozen dataclass (line 40)
- [x] `matches()` logic correct: early return False when `self.exclude and node_ref_id in self.exclude` (line 52). The `and` short-circuits on None/empty tuple.
- [x] `_parse_node_matcher()` handles both string and list YAML input (lines 233-239), normalizes to tuple
- [x] Backward compatibility preserved: exclude=None by default
- [x] No bare excepts, no `Any` types, no `print()`/`breakpoint()`
- [x] 4 `# type: ignore` annotations all have specific mypy error codes ([call-overload], [arg-type]) for YAML object->int/float conversions
- [x] Uses `yaml.safe_load()`, not `yaml.load()`
- [x] Uses `pathlib.Path`, not `os.path`

### 2. rules.yml
- [x] Version 3 with tags block
- [x] 9 rules syntactically correct (3 require, 1 deny, 1 forbid_cycles, 1 layer, 1 cardinality, 2 forbid_import)
- [x] `service-needs-parent` uses `exclude: [beadloom]` correctly
- [x] Severity levels appropriate: error for structural rules, warn for cycles/layers/cardinality (known coupling)
- [x] forbid_import glob patterns match beadloom's `src/beadloom/{tui,onboarding}/**` -> `src/beadloom/infrastructure/**` structure

### 3. services.yml
- [x] Tags properly added to all relevant nodes (8 nodes have layer tags)
- [x] Tag assignments match rules.yml tags block exactly:
  - layer-service: cli, mcp-server, tui
  - layer-domain: context-oracle, doc-sync, graph, onboarding
  - layer-infra: infrastructure

### 4. Tests (test_rule_engine.py, test_integration_v1.py)
- [x] 10 exclude tests cover: single exclude, multiple excludes, None default, non-matching ref_id, combined with kind/tags, empty tuple, YAML string parse, YAML list parse, YAML not present
- [x] Integration tests verify: rules.yml version 3, >= 7 rules, self-lint 0 errors, self-lint --strict exit 0, graph completeness
- [x] Tests follow AAA pattern, use tmp_path fixtures

### 5. PRD Acceptance Criteria
- [x] NodeMatcher `exclude` filter implemented with tests
- [x] rules.yml upgraded from v1 to v3 (tags support)
- [x] Rules expanded from 4 to 9
- [x] `beadloom lint --strict` passes (verified by BEAD-04)
- [x] All existing tests pass + new tests

### 6. Rule Type Coverage (6 of 7)
Present in rules.yml:
- [x] require: domain-needs-parent, feature-needs-domain, service-needs-parent
- [x] deny: no-domain-depends-on-service
- [x] forbid_cycles: no-dependency-cycles
- [x] forbid_import: tui-no-direct-infra, onboarding-no-direct-infra
- [x] layer: architecture-layers
- [x] check (cardinality): domain-size-limit
Missing:
- [ ] forbid (ForbidEdgeRule — graph edge forbid)

### Observation (Minor — does NOT block)
The `forbid` rule type (ForbidEdgeRule) is not exercised in rules.yml. The engine supports it (parser + evaluator exist), but no rule instance uses it. The PRD says "covering all 7 types" but the task description says 9 rules across the types. Since 6 of 7 types are covered with 9 rules, and the forbid type is closely related to the deny type (one checks code_imports, the other checks graph edges), this is a minor gap — not a structural deficiency. Can be added in a follow-up if desired.

### Code Quality Summary
- No anti-patterns found (no Any, no bare except, no print/breakpoint, no import *)
- yaml.safe_load used throughout
- pathlib.Path used (no os.path)
- Frozen dataclasses for all rule types
- SQL uses parameterized queries

Review = OK. Proceed to tech-writer.

title: BEAD-04: Code review
description: Review BEAD-01 and BEAD-02 code. Check type safety, error handling, edge cases, test coverage. Verify no anti-patterns from CLAUDE.md section 7.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: Review = OK. Thorough review of BEAD-01 and BEAD-02 changes. All 31 tests pass. ruff and mypy clean.

## Type Safety
- All functions have proper type hints (Python 3.10+ union syntax used consistently)
- Return types match actual returns (list[Check], str | None, set[str], list[str], etc.)
- Optional parameters properly typed (project_root: Path | None in run_checks, dry_run: bool = False)
- TYPE_CHECKING imports used correctly for Path in scanner.py

## Error Handling
- File I/O: claude_md_path.is_file() + try/except OSError in both doctor.py and scanner.py
- AGENTS.md: same pattern — is_file() check before read
- Regex no-match: _extract_version_claim returns None; _extract_package_claims returns empty set
- importlib.metadata fallback to __version__ in _get_actual_version

## Edge Cases
- Missing CLAUDE.md: _check_agent_instructions returns empty list; refresh_claude_md returns []
- Missing AGENTS.md: silently skips MCP tool comparison
- Malformed markers: _parse_markers handles unclosed markers (breaks loop)
- No section 0.1: _auto_insert_markers returns text unchanged
- No packages found: _get_actual_packages returns empty set for nonexistent paths

## Test Coverage
- BEAD-01: 16 tests — covers extractors (happy + empty), runtime introspection, full integration (version match/mismatch, no files, package mismatch, AGENTS.md)
- BEAD-02: 15 tests — covers parse_markers (pairs, no markers, multiple, unclosed), auto_insert (insert, no-op, already exists), render_section, refresh_claude_md (markers, dry-run, auto-insert, no-change, missing file, content preservation)
- Good use of tmp_path fixtures and unittest.mock.patch

## Anti-patterns
- No bare print()/breakpoint() in new code (scanner.py print() hits are Rich console.print)
- No os.path — pathlib only
- No import *, no mutable defaults, no f-strings in SQL
- No bare except: — all exception handlers specify types

## Architecture
- doctor.py: fact extractors + _check_agent_instructions correctly placed in infrastructure
- scanner.py: marker parsing + refresh_claude_md correctly placed in onboarding
- scanner.py reuses _get_actual_packages/_get_actual_version from doctor.py (lazy import inside function to avoid circular deps)
- No circular imports: scanner -> doctor (one-way)
- Clean separation: doctor = read-only check, scanner = write/refresh
- Existing Check/Severity dataclasses reused

## Code Quality
- Functions follow SRP: each extractor does one thing; _parse_markers, _auto_insert_markers, _render_project_info_section, refresh_claude_md are cleanly separated
- Naming consistent (snake_case, _private prefix for internal helpers)
- Docstrings on all public and private functions
- One minor observation: _parse_markers returns tuple[str, int, int, str] — a NamedTuple would be more readable, but since it's private + well-documented, this is acceptable

## CLI Integration
- setup-rules --refresh and --dry-run flags properly wired with mutual validation (--dry-run requires --refresh)
- doctor command passes project_root to run_checks
- Clean SystemExit(1) on validation errors

No issues found. Code is clean and well-structured.

title: [BDL-028] BEAD-05: Code review
description: Review all code changes from BEAD-01,02,03,04. Check quality, patterns, edge cases.

comments.author: v.zoologov
comments.text:
COMPLETED: Review = OK. All 3 fixes + regression tests reviewed.

## Code Quality
- ruff check: All checks passed
- mypy: no issues found in 22 source files
- pytest: 347 passed in 41.26s
- No new print/breakpoint/bare-except/import-*/unjustified-type-ignore

## Fix #58 (Threading error on quit)
- app.py: _shutting_down flag added as bool attribute, set in on_unmount() BEFORE worker.cancel() — correct ordering prevents race window
- file_watcher.py: getattr(app, '_shutting_down', False) check before post_message + try/except RuntimeError around post_message — double defense, both paths covered
- Thread safety: CPython bool read/write is atomic under GIL, sufficient for a flag pattern. No need for threading.Event here.
- Verdict: CORRECT, minimal, focused

## Fix #59 (Downstream dependents empty)
- dependency_path.py: _cached_text pattern pre-renders in show_downstream()/show_upstream() via _build_text(), render() returns cached snapshot
- set_provider() correctly invalidates cache (_cached_text = None) so next render recomputes
- No memory leak risk: Text objects are lightweight, replaced on each show_* call
- Verdict: CORRECT, clean solution to Textual deferred-refresh race

## Fix #60 (Explorer broken after empty visit)
- explorer.py: on_screen_resume() checks app._selected_ref_id, calls set_ref_id() only when pending != current — prevents redundant reloads
- Guards: returns early when app is None or pending is empty
- action_switch_screen already calls set_ref_id; on_screen_resume is a safety net for edge cases — no double-load risk because of the != check
- Verdict: CORRECT, covers all navigation paths (dashboard->explorer, doc_status->explorer)

## Tests
- 10+ new tests across 3 test classes (TestFileWatcherShutdownFlag, TestDependencyPathCacheConsistency, TestExplorerScreenResume)
- AAA pattern followed consistently
- Edge cases covered: empty state, same-ref no-op, rapid switches, worker-cancelled, empty filtered batch, no-watcher/no-conn unmount
- Test names are descriptive and map to specific scenarios
- Integration tests verify full navigation flows (dashboard<->explorer with node selection)

## Additional Changes (UX polish, not bugs)
- Footer moved from App.compose to per-screen compose (DashboardScreen, ExplorerScreen, DocStatusScreen) — correct, each screen owns its Footer
- Custom action-bar Labels replaced with Textual Footer widget — consistent UX
- Container heights changed from 100% to 1fr — prevents Footer overlap, correct Textual layout
- Binding key_display values added for ? and / — cosmetic improvement
- Tab binding hidden (show=False) — reduces Footer clutter
- get_symbols() refactored from tree-sitter to SQLite code_symbols table — cleaner, no filesystem dependency
- Tests updated to match new Footer-based UI

## Summary
All changes are minimal, focused, correctly implemented, and well-tested. No issues found.

title: BUG: Multiple nodes referencing same doc - last writer wins
description: In _build_doc_ref_map (reindex.py), if two YAML nodes list the same doc in their docs[] array, the last node overwrites the mapping. E.g., both beadloom and context-oracle reference architecture.md, but only context-oracle gets the link. Fix: support many-to-many doc-node mapping, or warn on conflict.

title: BUG: Module-level beadloom annotations ignored by code_indexer
description: code_indexer.py only detects annotations immediately before def/class. Module-level annotations (# beadloom:domain=X at file top) are consumed by pending_annotation but reset by the next non-symbol node (docstring, imports). Fix: support module-level annotations that apply to ALL symbols in the file, OR document that annotations must be before def/class.

title: UX: hardcoded docs/ path in reindex - should be configurable
description: reindex.py line 183 hardcodes docs_dir = project_root / 'docs'. Users who use doc/, documentation/, or other directory names cannot use beadloom. Fix: read from config.yml or accept CLI parameter.

title: UX: ctx markdown formatter heading collision with chunk content
description: _format_markdown uses ### for chunk headers, but chunk content may also contain ### headings (from H3 in docs). This makes output visually confusing. Fix: use a different heading level (##) or separator format for chunk boundaries.

title: BUG: suggest_ref_id misses prefix matches for short strings
description: Levenshtein threshold max(len//2, 3) is too restrictive for prefix matches. 'mcp' suggests 'cli' instead of 'mcp-server' because edit distance to mcp-server is 7 > threshold 3. Fix: add prefix matching as a supplementary strategy alongside Levenshtein.
